votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by checking if the output list is exactly the sorted list of unique common elements from both input lists.

The Python code correctly computes the sorted list of unique common elements using set intersection and sorting, which exactly matches the Coq specification of containing common elements, being sorted, and having no duplicates.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by checking if the output list is exactly the sorted list of unique common elements from both input lists.

The Python spec computes the sorted unique common elements by taking the intersection of sets (ensuring elements are in both l1 and l2), converting to a list, and sorting. This matches the Coq spec which requires: (1) l_out contains exactly elements in both l1 and l2, (2) l_out is sorted, and (3) l_out has no duplicates. The Python implementation achieves all three conditions and will return the same judgment for any concrete inputs.
